channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Burnt Bus Stop, 8th June
item.description:
The bus stop was fire damaged but has not been repaired properly. There is still a smell of burned chemicals that might affect the environment. It also looks very neglected and still might be dangerous for small children or elderly people and it presents a health and safety risk. The bus stop needs to be fully replaced.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crystal Palace Park Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped porch door and frame plus cardboard boxes by trade waste bin, 8th June
item.description:
A porch door and large porch frame has been flytipped at the side of the Candy Box in Maple Crescent. There has been work being undertaken on the flat at the rear above the Candy Box Blackfen Parade. It appears that work is being carried out to the front entrance porch of the property. Furthermore a couple of large cardboard boxes have been left at the side of the trade bin belonging to the candy box instead of being placed in the bin. Can a council officer have a discreet word with the shop owner/landlord as this is unacceptable. Furthermore I would be grateful if you will make arrangements as soon as possible to remove all this rubbish and building waste. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Maple Crescent, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 8th June
item.description:
Graffiti

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Farmdale Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bunch of items on the street, 8th June
item.description:
I live in a nearby street and I always see fly-tipping outside of house number 50 Riverdale Road. It looks absolutely disgusting and takes up space.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Riverdale Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Recycling Electricals Bin, 8th June
item.description:
This pink bin is really useful for recycling small electricals. I am so glad it's been put here and I do use it. However it is being misused for non electrical recycling eg stuff which should go in the blue bins. Today a passer-by pulled the bin out and opened the back panel and took stuff out of it. Residents of the nearby blocks would not do this. It should not be used for people to take stuff out as it's for recycling. Please can this back panel be locked as any items can be deposited using the top of the bin. I suggest moving the bin further along nearer the corner and next to a blue bin to make it more difficult for inappropriate use.. Thank you.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Blissett Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Scooter speeding on pavement, 8th June
item.description:
Frequently occurring. Scooters riding on the pavement at full speed. Already had one seriously injured teen last year.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Valley Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped garden rubbish on the pavement, 8th June
item.description:
As above. Towards the London Lane end of Warner Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warner Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, 8th June
item.description:
Fly tipping on grass rear off 114 wren path se280dy

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Harrier Mews, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bones everywhere, 8th June
item.description:
Increase in food waste not being binned properly. Especially bones that are a choking hazard to dogs. This park is used for large gatherings especially on weekends and during nice weather. Can more be done to ensure people are throwing away their waste. Bones still need to be binned.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Georges Way, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking the pavement and a possible danger to pedestrians, 8th June
item.description:
Pavement partially blocked for months. Pipe held up blue rope. This does not look safe. See attached photos

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, High Street, Goddington, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping in residential area, 8th June
item.description:
Person with access to garage number 139 has a white van with plates GJ65 AHZ and has been dumping bulky residential waste in our (building's) parking area. I have pictures of the van , the rubbish they have dumped here and the number of the garage #139 (next to the dump site) of Lucey Way that they have been using. White van, two males, dark skin, in their 50s I'd imagine. This area has been an absolute dumping ground as you'd probably already know. Please do something about this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lucey Way, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed no MOT dumped car, 8th June
item.description:
Untaxed no MOT dumped car - looks completely unroad worthy and a danger to the public that has been dumped in the car park. It has had no MOT or tax for a year and has been dumped for over a week meaning the owner has been driving this around

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bakers Hill, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandond vehicle, 8th June
item.description:
This vehicle, KN67 GUE, has been abandoned on Morley Road in Chadwell Heath for the last 6 months.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Lime Bike, 8th June
item.description:
Lime bike, dumped in the street. I do not believe that Lime bikes operate in the Greenwich borough

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Well Hall Road, Well Hall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip hazard, 8th June
item.description:
There is a wire coming from 78 Belmont Road into the pavement and many people have tripped over it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Belmont Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London

item.description:
Pieces of furniture and bed frames left under Mandeville house. Pls remove

item.title: More filth dumped by idiots, 8th June
item.description:
Just dumped outside the house Get a job!!!
